title: Citrusy Velvety Sicilian Sardines Risotto
description: A creamy risotto infused with bright lemon and orange zest, briny sardines, and crisp rapeseed oil, delivering a velvety texture and true Sicilian citrus sparkle.
instructions: Heat the vegetable broth to a gentle simmer and keep warm.
In a large skillet, heat rapeseed oil over medium heat. Add the onion and garlic mixture, sautéing until translucent and fragrant, about 3–4 minutes.
Stir in Arborio rice, coating the grains and toasting slightly, about 2 minutes.
Pour in the white wine, stirring until the liquid is fully absorbed.
Add warm broth one ladleful at a time, stirring frequently and allowing each addition to be absorbed before adding the next. Continue until the rice is creamy and al dente, roughly 18–20 minutes.
While the risotto cooks, drain the sardines, reserving a tablespoon of their oil. Roughly break the sardines into large flakes and set aside.
Add the citrus zest and juice to the risotto, stir to combine, and let cook for another minute.
Fold in the sardines, capers, and grated Pecorino, stirring until the cheese melts and the risotto achieves a velvety consistency. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Remove from heat, garnish with chopped parsley, and serve immediately.
